questions.q: If the sum of the first n terms of a G.P. is Sn = a(r^n - 1)/(r - 1) = 8n(n - 1), find the first term and the common ratio.
The sum of the first three terms of the sequence 1 + 2x + 3x^2 + 4x^3 + ... is (1 + 2x + 3x^2) + (4x^3 + 5x^4 + 6x^5). Find the sum of the first four terms of the sequence.
If a G.P. has a common ratio of 2 and the sum of its first three terms is 21, find the first term of the G.P.
In a G.P. with 5 terms, the sum of the first 3 terms is 13 and the sum of the last 2 terms is 80. Find the first term and the common ratio.
Show that 2 + 6 + 12 + ... + 6n is a G.P. and find its nth term and sum to n terms.
If the 5th term of a G.P. is 32 times the 2nd term, find the first term and the common ratio.
In a G.P., the sum of the first two terms is 5 and the sum of the first four terms is 49. Find the first term and the common ratio.
If the 7th term of a G.P. is 729 times the 3rd term, find the first term and the common ratio.
The sum of the first n terms of an arithmetic-geometric progression is 2n - 3 + 5n^(2) - 3n^(3) + 3^(n-4) n^(n-2). Find the first term and the common ratio.
If the sum of the first n terms of a G.P. is 10n + 2n^(2) - 3, find the first term and the common ratio.
